content: EC-VERIFY-FIRST-001: confirmed Maker re-derived every finding from live disk/git state (file re-read, domain-scope re-read, git show run) rather than relying on the cto trail's own self-report as sufficient. Pass.

Domain-boundary check (Law 4): confirmed via Maker's direct quote of cto/domain-scope/SKILL.md that 'PMCR-O loop/skill-pack design and validation' is explicitly in cto's Owns. Neither edited artifact touches another domain's own SKILL.md. No Law 4 crossing. Pass.

Work-quality check: independently re-verified (not re-trusted) zero remaining '../../' substrings, correct heading rename, and brief-session.md's frontmatter/style match to siblings. Agrees with cto/6df5bc78's own checker findings, but this agreement is now based on this cycle's own independent read, not an inherited assumption. Pass.

HIL-self-seal-correctness check (this cycle's core purpose, per approve-initiative.md): re-applied hil-gating.md's TYPE1 list against cto/6df5bc78's actual cycle actions -- no skills.json/marketplace.json write, no entry rename/removal, no git commit/push within that cycle. Agree none of the TYPE1 triggers fired during the cycle itself, and the human's dispatch wording was concrete enough (named file, named rename, named new command file) to match this Colony's own established pre-authorization precedent (trail cto/67f24fd5). Pass -- cto's self-seal of 'accept' was appropriately reasoned, not a boundary violation needing retroactive correction.
Git-scope check: 'git show --stat cba3315' output independently confirms exactly the two intended files, no scope creep. Pass.

Open items still unresolved by this review, correctly not silently closed: (1) commit cba3315 not yet pushed to origin; (2) .pmcro/trails/ tracking status undecided; (3) brief-session.md authored but never executed, so .agents/memory-bank/ does not exist yet; (4) the second occurrence (not yet EC-threshold) of literal-vs-intended '<repo_path>/.agents/rules/' wording, logged in cto/6df5bc78's own reflect.jsonl. None of these block approval of the initiative as already landed -- they are follow-on decisions, not defects in what was done.
